content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTICEs C ouulsSIoN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 18,2006
Jim Leland, Councilmember
City of Novato
115 Emerson Street
Novato, Califomia 94949
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-06-154
Dear Mr. Leland:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
May you participate in the decision regarding the future use of Todd Road?
CONCLUSION
You may participate in the decision so long as the decision will not have a
foreseeable materiai financial effect on your economic interest.
FACTS
You serve as a Novato City council member. At the August 8,2006 meeting, the
city council is scheduled to hold a public hearing on the relocation of tnrck traffic from
Todd Road. In a telephone message left on August 7,2006, you stated that the matter
would be deferred to the next regularly scheduled city council meeting.
Todd Road is a private road which provides access for construction and
maintenance of several public agency project sites located on the former Hamilton Air
Force Base. Todd Road is not a road available for the public's use. Todd Road is located
within 20 feet of the rear of 50+ single family homes. Residents have complained of
excessive noise and pollution levels from large diesel trucks which utilize the road. The
I Government Code sections 81000 - 9l}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code ofRegulations.
ary4,tEr


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 2
City of Novato, in cooperation with several agencies, is considering relocating the
construction truck traffic to a new, temporary haul road which will be located away from
any homes.
You describe three economic interests thatmay be impacted by the decision on
Todd Road.
o You have apersonal residence located more than 500 feet from Todd Road.
o You are also areal estate agent, but, none of your clients own property within
500 feet ofTodd Road.
o Finally, your employer, Bradley Real Estate ("Bradley'), has a client within
500 feet of Todd Road.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest. In order to determine whether the
prohibition in section 87100 applies to a given decision, regulation 18700 provides the
following eight-step analysis.
1 and 2. Are you a "public official" and wiII you be making, participating in
making, or influencing a governmental decision?
As a member of the Novato City Council, you are a "member, officer, employee
or consultant of a state or local government agency" and, therefore, are subject to the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048; regulation 18701(a).) A
public official "makes a goveflrmental decision" when the official, acting within the
authority of his or her position, votes on amatter, obligates or commits his or her agency
to any course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her
agency. (Section 87100; regulation 18702.1.) A public official "participates in making a
governmental decision" when, acting within the authority of his or her position and
without significant intervening substantive review, the official negotiates, advises or
makes recommendations to the decisionmaker regarding the govemmental decision.
(Section 87100; regulation 18702.2.) A public official is affempting to use his or her
official position to influence a decision before his or her own agency if, for the purpose
of influencing the decision, the official contacts or appears before any member, officer,
employee, or consultant of his or her agency. (Section 87100; regulation 18702.3.) You
wish to make and participate in making decisions concerning the use of Todd Road.
3. What are your economic interests -- the possible sources of a conflict of interest?
Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a
governmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 3
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on any of tfre oifi"iul,r rconomic
interests, described as follows:
o A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or
she has a direct or indirect investment of $2,000 or more (Settion 57103(a);
Regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner,
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management (Section Si t Of 1a;;
regulation I 8703. 1 (b));
o A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she
has a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(b);
regulation 18703.2);
o A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prioi
to the decision (Section 87103(c); regulation 1g703.3);
o A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her
if the gifts aggregate to $360 or more within 12 months prior to the decision
(Section 871 03(e); regulation 187 03.4);
o A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances,
including those of his or her immediate family -- this iythe "personal financial
effects" rule (Section 87103; regulation 18703.5).
Regulation 18703.3(c) contains disclosure and disqualification requirements for
any public official who receives commission income for services rendered as a real estate
agent. Note that a number ofparties in a real estate transaction are considered sources of
income pursuant to this regulation. Regulation 18703.3(c) @(C)provides, with respect to
real estate agents, that the following are all sources of the commission income in a given
transaction:
"(i) The broker and brokerage business entity under whose
auspices the agent works;
"(ii) The person the agent represents in the transaction; and
"(iii) Any person who receives a finder's or other referral fee for
referring aparty to the transaction to the broker, or who makes a referral
pursuant to a contract with the broker.,,


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 4
Applied to your facts, we identifu both Bradl ey, and the clients y ou represenl as sources
of income to you as discussed below. Clients of Bradley you do not ripresent would not
be considered sources of income to you.
Bradley
You have an economic interest in Bradley because you are employed by Bradley
(Section 87103(c) & (d)) and Bradley is a .our"" of incorne to you, assuming you have
received income from Bradley which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior
to the time the relevant decision will be made. You note that Bradleyhas a client who
lives within 500 feet of Todd Road, but that you did not represent that client. The client
would not be a source of income to you and we do not discuss that client as a separate
economic interest any further.
Clients
You note that you also have clients that you represented in the jurisdiction,
although none of them own property with 500 feet of Todd Road.
Personal Residence
Under section 87103(b) of the Act and regulation 78703.2, a public official has an
economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect interest of
$2'000 or more. Presumably, you have at least a $2,000 economic interest in your single-
familyhome.
Personal Ftnances
A public official always has an economic interest in his or herpersonal finances.
A governmental decision will have an effect on this economic interestlf the decision will
result in his or her personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities or those of his or her
immediate family increasing or decreasing. (Regulation 18703.5.) Generally however,
when determining whether a govemmental decision has a material financial "if""t 
or, u
public official's economic interest in his or herpersonal finances, neither a financial
effect on the value of real property owned directly or indirectly by the official, nor a
financial effect on the gross revenues, expenses, or value of assets and liabilities of a
business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment interest shall be
considered. Consequently, we will not further analyzepersonal financial effects.


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 5
4. Are your economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the governmental
decision?
Bradley and Clients You Represented
"(a) Aperson, including business entities..., is directly involved in
a decision before an official's agency when that person, either directly or
by an agent:
"(1) Initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request or;
"(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject of the proceeding
conceming the decision before the official or the official's agency. A
person is the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the issuance,
renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other
entitlernent to, or contract with, the subject person.,, (Regulation
18704.1(a).)
Because neither Bradley nor any of your clients have initiated the Todd Road
proceeding, are not named parties in nor is the subject of the decision, Bradley and your
customers are not directly involved in the decision. If a source of income is not directly
involved in a governmental decision, as here, the materiality standards for an indirectly
involved business entity apply. (Regulation l\704.t(b)(t) and (2).)
Personal Residence
Real property is directly involved in a govemmental decision if anypart of that
real property is located within 500 feet of the boundaries (or proposed boundaries) of the
real property which is the subject of the govemmental decision. You indicate thai your
personal residence is more than 500 feet from Todd Road. Thus, your property is
indirectly involved in this decision.
5 and 6. Determine which materialify standards apply and if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the applicable materiality standards will be met?
Bradley
The financial effect of a governmental decision on a small business entity, which
is not directly involved in the decision, is material if it is reasonably foreseeable that:
"(A) The governmental decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the business entity's gross revenues for a fiscal year in the
amount of $20,000 or more; or,


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 6
"(B) The governmental decision will result in the business entity
incurring or avoiding additional expenses or reducing or eliminating
existing expenses for a fiscal year in the amount of $5,000 or more; or,
"(C) The governmental decision will result in an increase or
decrease in the value of the business entity's assets or liabilities of
$20,000 or more." (Regulation I 8705. 1 (cX4).)
An effect upon economic interests is considered "reasonably foreseeable" if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. (Regulation 18706(a).) A financial effect
need not be certain to be considered reasonably foreseeable, but it must be more than a
mere possibility. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.) The determination of
whether or not a financial effect is reasonably foreseeable is necess arily afactaal
question.
Thus, for example, if it was reasonably foreseeable that a decision will affect
Bradley's listing (or listings) of property near Todd Road such that the revenue Bradley
receives will be increased by $20,000 in a fiscal year, the matenality standard would be
met.
Your Clients
Your clients who have paid you $500 or more in the past 12 months are also
considered your sources of income. You note that none of your clients own property
within 500 feet of Todd Road. The test for sources of income that areindividuals and
indirectly involved in a government decision in regulation 18703.3(bX3) provides as
follows:
"The effect of a decision is material as to an individual who is a
source of income to an offici al if any of the following applies:
"(A) The decision will affect the individual's income, investments,
or other tangible or intangible assets or liabilities (other than real property)
by $1,000 or more; or
"(B) The decision will affect the individual's real property interest
in a manner that is considered material under Title 2, california code of
Regulations, sections I87 05 .2(b)."
Assuming the standards set forth in regulation 18703.3 are not met, the client will
not be materially affected by the decision.


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 7
Personal Residence
Regulation 18705.2(b)(1) sets forth the proper mateiality standards to apply
when an official's real property interest is indirectly involved in a governmental ae"ision.
The financial effect of a governmental decision on real property indirectly involved in the
govemmental decision is presumed not to be material, absent a showing that there are
specific circumstances regarding the governmental decision, its financiul eff""t, and the
nature of the official's property, which make it reasonably foreseeable that the decision
will have amateial financial effect on the official's property. Specific circumstances
that should be considered include, but are not limited to, circumstances where the
decision affects:
"(A) The development potential or income producing potential of
the real property in which the official has an economic interest;
"(B) The use of the real property in which the official has an
economic interest;
"(c) The character of the neighborhood including, but not limited
to, substantial effects on: traffic, view, pivacy, intensity of use, noise
levels, air emissions, or similar traits of the neighborhood." (Regulation
18705.2(bX1).)
As noted above, this determination is necessarily a factual one to be made by an
official. The Commission is not the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Ogleiby
(t97s) I FPPC Ops. 71.)
7 and 8. Exceptions
Even if a material financial effect on a public official's economic interest is
reasonably foreseeable, he or she still may not be disqualified if the financial effect of the
governmental decision on the public official's economic interest is indistinguishable from
the effect on the public generally (section 87|l3;regulation 1S700(bX7l u"A f g707(a)) or
the official is legally required to participate (section 87l}3;regulation f870S). you have
not presented any facts indicating that either of these exceptions is applicable in this
instance.


File No. A-06-154
Page No. 8
Should you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(e16) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

foreseeable materiai financial effect on your economic interest.

QUESTION: May you participate in the decision regarding the future use of Todd Road?

CONCLUSION: You may participate in the decision so long as the decision will not have a
foreseeable materiai financial effect on your economic interest.
